channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: Aluminum Fell to 3,575.20 USD/T on May 12, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices declined to 3,575.20 USD/T, down 0.09% from the previous day. Over the past month, aluminum's price has fallen 1.42%, though it remains significantly higher at 43.53% above year-ago levels.

item.title: HRC Steel Falls to 1,129.04 USD/T
item.description: Hot-Rolled Coil Steel declined 0.52% to 1,129.04 USD/T on May 12, 2026. Despite the daily decline, HRC Steel has gained 5% over the past 4 weeks and reached its highest level since January 2024 at 1,135.00 USD/T.

item.title: Steel Market Update: Prices Increase $5-25 per Short Ton
item.description: Steel prices increased between $5-25 per short ton from the previous week and are $30-65 per short ton higher than one month ago. The market continues to show upward momentum in pricing.

item.title: Copper Nears Record High as Traders Tune Out Trump's Iran Rejection
item.description: Copper prices moved close to record highs as traders focused more on demand and supply fundamentals than on geopolitical headlines. The article suggests market participants were largely discounting near-term Middle East risk in pricing.

item.title: Copper price nears record on China demand, supply worries
item.description: Copper rallied near record levels amid strong demand expectations from China and concerns about supply constraints. The report frames the market as tight, with bullish fundamentals outweighing short-term volatility.

item.title: Copper: Prices hold near records despite Iran risks
item.description: Copper prices remained elevated even as geopolitical risks from Iran continued to influence broader markets. The piece emphasizes that supply-demand dynamics were keeping copper near historic highs.

item.title: Nickel's Long-Term Bull Cycle Gains Momentum Amid ...
item.description: This video discusses the nickel market’s relative price stability and the role of supply management in Indonesia and the Philippines. It also highlights project permitting progress in Canada and emerging deep-sea mining plans that could affect future nickel supply and capacity.

item.title: Nickel Enters a New Era as Indonesia Tightens Supply and Prices ...
item.description: The video argues that nickel pricing is being reshaped by tighter Indonesian supply controls and rising production costs. It also notes inventory declines and higher sulfur costs as factors limiting output and supporting prices.

item.title: West Asia Has 10% Global Aluminium Capacity, Most Remains...
item.description: Nirmal Bang Securities analyst Kunal Shah discusses aluminum prices at 4-year highs, with Goldman Sachs and Citi projecting prices could reach 3,600 in the next 3 months. Supply concerns from the Middle East and China's planned smelting capacity cuts are driving bullish price trends.

item.title: Century Aluminum sees higher demand, restarts capacity, and plans 750,000 tonne smelter
item.description: Century Aluminum, with total production capacity of 770,000 tonnes per year, produced approximately 638,000 tonnes of primary aluminum in 2025. The company is responding to higher demand by restarting capacity and planning expansion.

item.title: Scientists create a new revolutionary aluminum that could slash industrial costs and accelerate the energy transition
item.description: Scientists have developed a new aluminum with innovative properties that could redefine production processes and significantly reduce dependence on critical metals. This breakthrough technology has the potential to substantially lower industrial costs while supporting the energy transition.

item.title: Steel Prices Rise 0.06% to 3,211 CNY/T
item.description: Steel prices in China rose to 3,211 CNY/T with a 0.06% increase from the previous day. Over the past month, steel prices have climbed 4.19%, and are up 2.36% compared to the same period last year.

item.title: US Steel Mill Outages Unlikely to Shift Market
item.description: Steel mill outages through the remainder of 2026 are expected to remove nearly 600,000 short tons of flat-rolled production capacity. Industry analysts believe these outages will have limited impact on overall market dynamics.

item.title: Copper Futures Reach Fresh All-Time Highs
item.description: Copper futures climbed to around $6.6 per pound, reaching fresh all-time highs on the back of stronger Chinese demand and growing supply concerns. The rally reflects both increased global demand and tightening supply conditions.

item.title: Breaking down sudden surge of nickel prices
item.description: The London Metal Exchange suspended nickel trading after prices more than doubled to surpass $100,000 per metric ton due to extreme price moves in Asian trading hours. The LME stated that trading would be suspended for at least the remainder of the day.

item.title: Nickel rose to 19,097.75 USD/T
item.description: Nickel reached $19,097.75 per metric ton on May 13, 2026, up 0.91% from the previous day. Over the past month, nickel's price has risen 4.88% and is up 20.87% year-to-date.

item.title: New EV battery recycling process recovers 99% pure nickel and cobalt
item.description: Researchers have developed an eco-friendly recycling technology that extracts 99 percent pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries. This innovation addresses sustainability concerns in the EV battery supply chain by enabling high-purity material recovery from end-of-life batteries.

item.title: Cogne Acciai Speciali: 94% of energy from renewable sources, 93% permanent employment
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali highlighted its sustainability initiatives, reporting that 94% of energy used comes from renewable sources and 93% of employees have permanent contracts, positioning itself as a conscious industrial player.

item.title: Tenaris enters into definitive agreement to acquire GLGH Steel, LLC
item.description: Tenaris has announced a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of the share capital of GLGH Steel, LLC, a U.S.-based company. This acquisition represents a strategic expansion of Tenaris's operations in the steel sector.

item.title: Copper Cables: Antitrust Launches Investigation into Alleged Cartel
item.description: Italy's Antitrust authority has opened an investigation against major low-voltage copper cable manufacturers, including Prysmian, for allegedly participating in anti-competitive agreements involving price fixing and coordinated sales practices since 2005. The investigation involves nine companies and the Italian Association of Electrical Cable and Conductor Industries (AICE). Prysmian's stock price declined 1.26% following the news.

item.title: US Producer Prices Surge 6% in April on Rising Energy Costs from Mideast Conflict
item.description: Prices paid to US producers jumped 6% annually in April, the largest gain since December 2022, driven by elevated energy costs amid the Mideast Gulf war. The producer price index rose from a revised 4.3% in March, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This surge impacts steel and related sectors.

item.title: Nickel Rises 1.27% to $19,165 Per Tonne Amid Supply Disruptions
item.description: Nickel futures traded above $19,500 per tonne, nearing a two-year high, driven by mining quota cuts in Indonesia and sulfur shortages linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions. These supply constraints have tightened raw material availability and raised processing costs for nickel intermediates, reinforcing bullish momentum in the market.

item.title: Codelco output questioned over 20,000-tonne gap
item.description: Preliminary audit findings raised fresh governance concerns at Chile's state copper miner Codelco amid questions about a 20,000-tonne output gap. The report adds to wider scrutiny of Chilean copper production just as the market is focused on supply constraints and capacity issues.

item.title: 4 MIN AGO: Canada Uncovers Massive Aluminum Find
item.description: Canada has discovered a massive alumina/aluminum-related deposit in Saskatchewan, potentially transforming North America's supply chain, manufacturing, and critical minerals strategy. The find could impact global aluminum supply amid trade tensions and rising demand. Countries are closely watching as it addresses import dependence.

item.title: Steel Falls to 3209 CNY/T Amid Expectations of Seasonal Demand Slowdown
item.description: Steel prices fell to 3209 CNY/T on May 14, 2026, down 0.12% from the previous day, as rebar futures pulled back from nine-month highs due to anticipated seasonal slowdown in demand and softer downstream consumption. Mysteel's monthly report highlights pressure on construction steel prices from weakening supply-demand fundamentals. Over the past month, prices have risen 3.72%.

item.title: SMU Sheet and Plate Price Indices Rise to New Multi-Year Highs
item.description: All five of SMU's sheet and plate price indices increased this week, reaching new multi-year highs with gains of $5-25 per short ton from last week. Prices are now $30-65 per short ton higher than one month ago, signaling strong upward momentum in the US market.

item.title: Latest Copper News
item.description: Recent developments in copper mining emphasize sustainable practices and the exploration of new deposits. Chile, as the world's largest copper producer, is central to these efforts. This reflects the ongoing push for sustainability in the industry.

item.title: Nickel Falls 0.78% as Profit-Taking Emerges After Recent Rally
item.description: Nickel futures fell to $18,842.50 per tonne, down 0.78% from the previous day, as traders locked in gains following a sharp rally that brought prices near a two-year high of $19,600. The pullback reflects profit-taking rather than deterioration in fundamentals, with broader industrial metals showing mixed price action after a strong run across the base metals complex.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Market Update
item.description: Nickel futures are currently trading at $18,871.75, down from the previous close of $19,198.50. The market outlook remains bearish according to UBS, which warns of persistent surplus conditions expected through 2026 despite recent production cuts.

item.title: New recycling tech recovers nearly pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries
item.description: Researchers have developed an eco-friendly recycling technology that extracts 99 percent pure nickel and cobalt from waste batteries. This innovation addresses sustainability concerns in battery recycling and material recovery for the EV industry.

item.title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
item.description: The global nickel market is undergoing rapid changes with innovations in mining and refining aimed at reducing production costs and enhancing environmental sustainability. These developments represent a shift toward more responsible nickel production practices.

item.title: To Meet Demand, JSW Steel Plans Steel Capacity Increase of Nearly 17M Tons
item.description: JSW Steel is planning a major capacity expansion of nearly 17 million tons to keep up with strong demand. The company is allocating more than US$2.5 billion for capital projects tied to India’s infrastructure growth and rising steel consumption.

item.title: Copper futures drop amid Chilean mine cuts and AI demand
item.description: Copper futures fell about 1% despite ongoing worries about supply after Chile's three largest mines posted a year-over-year production decline of roughly 43,000 metric tons. The report also notes sulfuric acid shortages and rising input costs, which are adding pressure to producers.

item.title: Copper rally gains momentum as supply disruptions drive market
item.description: This market update says copper prices are being supported by supply disruption concerns alongside resilient Chinese demand. It also points to AI-related data center demand and the energy transition as major drivers behind the broader copper rally.

item.title: Global Copper Shortage: AI Demand vs Physical Supply Limits
item.description: This report argues that copper supply is being squeezed by structural mining disruptions just as demand rises from AI data centers, EVs, and wind turbines. It highlights production problems at major mines, including disruptions in Indonesia and persistent weakness at Codelco, alongside sharply higher copper prices.

item.title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
item.description: Fastmarkets reports that the copper market is being shaped by tight mine supply and record-high prices, with analysts warning of unsustainable deficits. The coverage also notes major production changes, including strong African output at Glencore and a steep drop in Ivanhoe Mines’ Kamoa-Kakula copper production.

item.title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid U.S. copper tariffs
item.description: Prysmian shares rose as investors expected the company to benefit from U.S. tariffs on copper. Reuters said Prysmian’s large U.S. business could gain because it sources copper locally and may be able to pass higher costs on to customers.

item.title: Aluminum fell to 3,564 USD/T on May 15, 2026, down 2.44% from the previous day
item.description: Aluminum prices declined sharply in the latest session, reflecting short-term weakness in the market. Trading Economics notes that prices have also fallen over the past month, even though they remain substantially higher than a year ago.

item.title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel rose to 1,134 USD/T, its highest level since January 2024. Trading Economics says the gain reflects continuing strength in the market, with HRC prices up over the past month and year.

item.title: NMDC Steel Attains Production Record Using Danieli QSP Technology
item.description: NMDC Steel reported a production record after using Danieli’s QSP technology at its thin-slab casting and rolling setup. The plant reached 100% of rated capacity, highlighting improved performance in steelmaking operations.

item.title: Raw steel output continues to rise in US
item.description: U.S. raw steel production continued to increase, with mill capability utilization reaching 81.4%, up from 80.4% the previous week and 76.6% a year earlier. The report points to strengthening domestic steel output and higher operating rates.

item.title: Copper extends retreat on faster inflation and stronger dollar
item.description: Copper weakened as inflation data and a stronger U.S. dollar pressured commodity prices. The market retreat highlighted sensitivity to macroeconomic conditions alongside demand expectations.

item.title: Copper hits record $6.39 as Chile faces Codelco audit scandal
item.description: Copper touched a record high amid concerns surrounding Chile’s Codelco and a related audit scandal. The story points to supply-side uncertainty adding to already strong copper market momentum.

item.title: Copper prices rise to record highs on AI demand
item.description: Copper climbed to record levels as AI-related infrastructure spending boosted demand expectations. The article links the metal’s strength to data center growth and electrification needs.

item.title: Copper price retreats from record close as purchases in China slow
item.description: Copper futures slipped after an eight-day rally as buying in China eased, even though broader supply tightness remains a major theme. The move highlights how near-record prices are being balanced by concerns about demand momentum and production constraints.

item.title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Nickel fell to $18,580 per tonne, down 1.90% on the day, as traders took profits after a strong rally to a recent two-year high near $19,600. Despite the pullback, the market remains supported by supply disruptions, Indonesia's quota cuts, and tighter raw material availability for downstream processors.

item.title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Nickel prices eased after a rally to a recent high, but the broader trend remained supported by supply disruptions and tighter raw material availability. The update highlights Indonesian quota cuts and sulfur-related processing constraints as key supply-side pressures.

item.title: Prysmian Daily News Update: EU industrial policy, cables exclusion, and market reaction
item.description: This update highlights Prysmian’s response to the EU’s proposed Industrial Acceleration Act, noting that cables were excluded from the initiative. It also frames the development in the context of broader pressure on the copper cable market and related industry policy discussions.

item.title: US flat steel price edges higher again, May scrap now adds to bullish fundamentals
item.description: U.S. flat steel prices moved higher again as scrap market conditions added support to already bullish fundamentals. The report points to firmer pricing momentum in the domestic sheet market.

item.title: Global View on Scrap: Turkey’s import prices move up, Asia remains stable
item.description: Scrap import prices in Turkey edged up, while Asian markets remained stable. The update reflects mixed regional conditions in the global scrap market and their impact on steel pricing.

item.title: Ex-Russia BPI prices supported by limited demand
item.description: Prices for ex-Russia billet and slab products were supported despite weak demand, suggesting constrained availability is helping prevent sharper declines. The article highlights how supply-side factors are influencing steel market trends.

item.title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Steel prices fell to 3,211 CNY/ton, down 0.86% on the day, after pulling back from recent highs. The decline was linked to expectations of seasonal demand slowdown and softer downstream consumption in China.

item.title: Copper extends retreat after record highs as traders take profits
item.description: Copper prices eased from record levels as traders locked in gains while reassessing supply and demand fundamentals. The market had recently been supported by strong expectations for future industrial demand, but short-term profit-taking pushed prices lower.

item.title: Prysmian Daily News Update
item.description: This internal Prysmian news bulletin highlights recent company developments and industry trends. The latest episode mentioned Prysmian's response to the exclusion of cables from the EU's proposed Industrial Acceleration Act, which may affect the sector’s regulatory outlook.

item.title: Price of Aluminum per Pound
item.description: Kitco’s live aluminum quote shows the metal trading around 1.6570 per pound, with a small daily decline. The page provides multi-market pricing context and realtime charts, useful for tracking intraday trend changes.

item.title: Aluminum Prices and Aluminum Futures Prices
item.description: Barchart offers updated aluminum futures prices, charts, and related market information. The listing indicates the market is focused on the active July 2026 contract, suggesting near-term trader attention is centered on the front-month future.

item.title: ALUMINIUM Charts and Quotes
item.description: TradingView’s aluminum page includes charting and recent technical commentary pointing to a possible bullish rebound from a key support area. The analysis suggests traders are watching whether buyers can defend the zone and reverse the recent weakness.

item.title: Aluminium faces 'black swan' supply shock, Mercuria says
item.description: Mercuria's top metals analyst says the global aluminium market is facing a 'black swan' event, with Middle East war disruptions triggering a supply shock. The report says this could create a deficit of roughly two million tons by year-end, implying tight supply and possible price pressure.

item.title: Latest Aluminium News
item.description: NewsNow aggregates the latest aluminium headlines, including items on Chinese output above cap due to price incentives, London Metal Exchange trading halts, and production cuts at Bahrain's smelter. Several of the listed stories focus on supply constraints, price strength, and production changes across the aluminium market.

item.title: Aluminum Industry News | Latest Headlines & Updates
item.description: This page compiles recent aluminium industry headlines, including reports on Rio Tinto's first-quarter 2026 production and Iranian aluminium output data. It serves as a running news feed on production, capacity, and broader market developments.

item.title: Aluminium Market Data
item.description: Fastmarkets' aluminium market page highlights recent analysis that metals markets are shifting toward a security-of-supply theme. It also notes active industry developments such as Tsingshan Holding Group exploring another aluminium smelter project in Indonesia.

item.title: Aluminum industries latest news
item.description: AL Circle provides a live news hub covering bauxite, alumina, primary aluminium, secondary aluminium, downstream, and end-user developments. The page is designed as a current feed for aluminium industry news and market updates.

item.title: Aluminum Market Trends
item.description: S&P Global's aluminium market trends page tracks key drivers affecting the US aluminum industry in 2026. It is framed around the company's Aluminum Symposium and focuses on broader market and capacity-related trends.

item.title: Paolo Rocca steps down as Tenaris CEO amid tensions with Milei
item.description: Paolo Rocca is stepping down as chief executive of Tenaris after more than two decades in the role. Gabriel Podskubka, currently COO, is set to take over as CEO while Rocca remains chairman and president of Techint Group.

item.title: News listing | Tenaris
item.description: Tenaris’s newsroom highlights recent company updates, including a leadership change, a geothermal partnership, and an acquisition agreement in Romania. The listing provides links to several current corporate announcements relevant to the company’s steel pipe business.

item.title: Copper futures dropped to near $6.3 per pound as elevated prices discouraged buying activity in China
item.description: Copper futures slipped for a second consecutive session as high prices curbed buying interest in China. Despite the pullback, the outlook for Chinese demand remains resilient, supported by clean energy and technology-related consumption.

item.title: Copper prices surge to record $6.5 per pound amid tight supply and AI-fueled demand
item.description: Copper hit a record high as traders focused on tight supply conditions and growing demand linked to artificial intelligence infrastructure. The article frames the rally as part of a broader bullish trend driven by long-term electrification and technology demand.

item.title: Copper futures price today
item.description: Copper futures were quoted lower, reflecting a pullback from recent highs. Reuters-linked market coverage highlighted supply-side developments, including the planned restart of Codelco’s El Teniente smelter after a collapse-related shutdown.

item.title: Copper market news and forecasts
item.description: Fastmarkets' copper coverage includes several recent supply-side developments, including strong African output from Glencore and lower production at Kamoa-Kakula. The updates underscore how mine performance and sulfuric acid shortages are shaping current copper supply conditions.

item.title: Industry Data
item.description: AISI reports that domestic raw steel production in the U.S. for the week ending May 16, 2026 was 1,898,000 net tons, with capability utilization at 82.2 percent. This indicates stronger recent mill operating rates.

item.title: SteelRadar homepage update featuring steel news and pricing
item.description: SteelRadar’s homepage is dated May 17, 2026 and highlights recent iron and steel industry news and prices. The visible item in the results mentions Metal Park launching 30,000-ton cut-and-bend rebar capacity, which is relevant to steel production capacity, but the snippet does not provide a fuller article excerpt.

item.title: Italy's Prysmian to Buy Encore Wire in $4.2B Deal
item.description: Prysmian announced a major acquisition of Texas-based Encore Wire, a move aimed at strengthening its North American presence. While not limited to the past 24 hours in the provided results, it is a relevant recent business development for the company and cable market.

item.title: Copper: Tariff risk fuels rally
item.description: Copper prices remained supported by trade-policy concerns, with tariff risk contributing to bullish sentiment. The piece suggests market participants are pricing in continued supply and policy uncertainty.

item.title: Aluminium recycling sector flags EU ETS risks, surge in copper prices too
item.description: The article notes that rising copper prices are affecting broader metals and recycling markets. It also connects the move to policy and cost pressures in Europe, where emissions-related risks are influencing industrial inputs.

item.title: News & Insights - Aluminum Market Update
item.description: CRU’s news page includes aluminum market updates such as Hydro’s Q1 results, where higher prices supported margins despite weaker North American extrusion demand. The item is relevant to current production trends, capacity utilization, and regional demand conditions.

item.title: Ball Corporation reports record 2025 results as aluminium sustainability metrics advance
item.description: Ball Corporation says its 2025 annual and sustainability report shows both record financial results and progress on environmental goals. The company reported that 74% of the aluminum used in its global beverage packaging business came from recycled sources in 2025, highlighting ongoing sustainability improvements in aluminum packaging.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 18.05.2026
item.description: SteelRadar’s latest daily news page highlights recent developments in the global iron and steel market. The page aggregates industry updates, pricing news, and commentary, but the snippet available does not show a specific article headline focused on production or capacity changes.

item.title: Tenaris acquires Artrom Steel Tubes for EUR86 million
item.description: Tenaris announced a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of Artrom Steel Tubes SA for EUR86 million on a cash-free and debt-free basis. Artrom is a steel tubes company, and the deal is expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2026. This is the most relevant recent item tying Tenaris to steel operations in Italy/Europe in the past day.

item.title: Aluminium Price Today
item.description: Investing.com shows aluminum futures at 3,561.65, unchanged from the previous close. The page also references market commentary about Comex aluminum premiums easing on tariff rollback expectations, suggesting tariff policy remains an active driver of pricing.

item.title: Smelter closure to wipe nearly 30% of US aluminum capacity
item.description: Fastmarkets reports that Magnitude 7 Metals planned to curtail operations at its Marston, Missouri smelter because high energy costs made the plant financially unfeasible. The closure would remove a large share of U.S. primary aluminum smelting capacity and leave only four primary smelters operating in the country.

item.title: Steel prices retreat from nine-month highs as seasonal slowdown weighs on demand
item.description: Steel rebar futures fell below CNY 3,200 per ton after reaching nine-month highs, as expectations of a seasonal slowdown in demand and softer downstream consumption pressured the market. Mysteel’s latest monthly report also pointed to weakening supply-demand fundamentals that could keep construction steel prices under pressure in China this month.

item.title: HRC Steel slips from recent highs after strong run
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel prices eased to 1,128.02 USD/T, down from a recent high of 1,143.00 USD/T, though prices remain elevated versus recent periods. The move suggests some cooling after the recent rally in steel prices.

item.title: Copper futures slip to near two-week lows as equities sell off; China demand weighs on prices
item.description: Copper futures fell to around $6.2 per pound, their lowest level in nearly two weeks, as a broader selloff in equities pressured commodity markets. The report also notes that elevated copper prices have discouraged buying activity in China, adding to the weakness.

item.title: Copper Futures Price Today
item.description: Copper futures were last quoted at 6.2530, down from a previous close of 6.3340. The page also highlights Reuters coverage that Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter is set to restart after a collapse, a development that could affect near-term supply conditions.

item.title: Nickel futures extend losses as softer ore and sulfate prices weigh on sentiment
item.description: Nickel futures traded around $18,500 per tonne, extending losses toward a one-month low. The report says weaker nickel ore prices in the Philippines and easing nickel sulfate prices have reduced near-term support and reinforced expectations of lower production costs across the supply chain.

item.title: Nickel price update: supply disruptions keep bullish momentum intact
item.description: Nickel prices were also reported above $19,500 per tonne, near a two-year high, as supply disruptions and tighter raw material availability continued to support the market. Indonesia's mining quota cuts and a sulfur shortage linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions added to cost pressures and output constraints.

item.title: HRC Steel Price Climbs to Highest Since December 2023
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel prices rose to 1,135 USD/T on May 19, marking a 0.18% increase from the previous day and reaching the highest level since December 2023. The data also shows steel prices have gained over the past month, reflecting continued upward momentum in the market.

item.title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
item.description: Aluminum prices fell as traders reacted to reports that President Donald Trump may narrow tariffs on some aluminum and steel products. The move raised expectations that some import levies could be rolled back, pressuring metal prices and weighing on broader industrial metals.

item.title: Nickel rose to 18,957.25 USD/T on May 20, 2026, up 0.94% from the previous day
item.description: Nickel prices continued to strengthen, reaching 18,957.25 USD/T and posting a modest daily gain. The market has also risen 4.45% over the past month and 21.29% over the past year, suggesting a broader upward trend in nickel pricing.

item.title: Nickel rose to 18,702.75 USD/T on May 20, 2026, up 0.23% from the previous day
item.description: A separate Trading Economics update shows nickel trading at 18,702.75 USD/T, reflecting a smaller day-over-day increase. This indicates the market remains elevated and active, with prices holding near recent highs.

item.title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
item.description: Indonesia has reportedly ordered PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine, to sharply reduce its ore production quota this year. Bloomberg says the quota could fall to 12 million tons from 42 million tons in 2025, as the country looks to support global nickel prices. The company says it plans to seek a revision.

item.title: Nickel Futures Rebound on Reports of Additional Output Reductions
item.description: Nickel prices recovered after reports of further production cuts, with futures moving back above $18,800 per ton. The move reflects growing attention on supply discipline and its effect on market balances.

item.title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs
item.description: Prysmian shares climbed after market commentary suggested the Italian cable maker could benefit from US copper tariffs. The article notes that investors viewed Prysmian as well-positioned in the cable sector amid changing trade conditions.

item.title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Copper futures were trading around $6.28 per pound, with the prior session showing a 2% gain. The update attributes support to improved global risk appetite amid renewed market optimism.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today
item.description: Investing.com provides the latest nickel futures quote along with the prior close and trading range information. The page is useful for monitoring short-term market movement and intraday volatility.

item.title: Prysmian | Cables, Energy & Telecom Solutions
item.description: This is Prysmian's official corporate site rather than a news article. It highlights the company's role as a global cable manufacturer and provides background on its energy and telecom solutions business.

item.title: HRC Steel Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel rose to 1,146 USD/T, its highest level since December 2023. The move reflects continued strength in steel pricing over the past several weeks, suggesting firm demand and/or constrained supply.

item.title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
item.description: The article reviews recent U.S. steel market conditions, noting capacity utilization has slipped below the administration’s 80% goal but has shown signs of stabilizing in recent weeks. It also highlights improving weekly production and utilization rates, suggesting potential tailwinds as demand recovers into the second half of the year.

item.title: United States Steel Production
item.description: Trading Economics reports that U.S. steel production rose to 7.2 million tonnes in March 2026 from 6.5 million tonnes in February 2026. The page provides historical context and tracks production trends over time using World Steel Association data.

item.title: Nickel futures recover after reports of additional output reductions
item.description: Nickel prices moved back above $18,800 per ton after reports of further supply reductions, extending a recent rebound from a near four-week low. The move reflects market sensitivity to potential production cuts and tightening supply expectations. The article focuses on pricing, but the driver is directly tied to production and capacity news.
